title: Grief Experience Reflection
description: This gentle reflection invites you to notice how grief is showing up in your life — physically, emotionally, cognitively, and behaviorally. It is not a diagnostic tool or a score to compare against. Its purpose is self-awareness and self-compassion.
instructions: For each statement below, choose the response that best describes your experience since your loss. There are no right or wrong answers. This is simply a way to notice and name what you're going through.

questions.text: I have felt physically exhausted or drained, even after rest
My sleep has been disrupted (trouble falling asleep, waking at night, or sleeping too much)
Grief comes in waves — sometimes intense, sometimes quieter
I experience strong yearning or longing for who or what I've lost
I have difficulty concentrating, making decisions, or thinking clearly
I feel guilt or regret about things I did or didn't do
I have withdrawn from people or activities I used to enjoy
Daily tasks and routines feel much harder than before

bands.label: Lighter grief presence
bands.description: Grief may be present in quieter ways right now. This is a valid place to be — grief doesn't always look intense. The tools in this course can support you wherever you are.

bands.label: Moderate grief presence
bands.description: Grief is affecting several areas of your life. This is a very common experience. The self-care, coping, and support strategies in this course are designed for exactly this. Be gentle with yourself.

bands.label: Significant grief presence
bands.description: Grief is weighing heavily on you right now. This level of impact is understandable, especially with significant loss. Please consider reaching out to a supportive person or professional alongside this course — you don't have to carry this alone.

bands.label: Intense grief presence
bands.description: You're carrying a very heavy load right now. Your pain is real and valid. We strongly encourage you to connect with a grief-informed therapist or counselor who can provide personalized support. If you're having thoughts of self-harm, please contact the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline (call or text 988) immediately.

functionalImpairmentQuestion.text: Overall, how much has your grief interfered with your ability to manage daily life?

disclaimer: This reflection is for self-awareness only. It is not a clinical diagnostic tool and does not assess for Prolonged Grief Disorder or any other condition. Only a trained clinician can make such assessments. If you are having thoughts of ending your life, call or text 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline) immediately.
